search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae swollen eyelid telehealth guideline stye PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine selection and doses were outside documentation scope.
Procedures and urgency remain clinician decisions.
Non-official summaries were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for guideline-described stye or hordeolum and selected red-eye warnings; other eyelid masses, orbital disease, trauma, and paediatric qualifiers are not fully covered.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth history and referral prompts; physical eye examination, diagnosis, medicine, procedure, and urgency remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai Health Authority telehealth services.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 swollen-eyelid and conjunctivitis PDFs, due for revision in 2029, were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA swollen-eyelid or conjunctivitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports stye or hordeolum identity, eyelid redness or swelling, assessed eyelid findings, and clinician-documented management or eyelid-hygiene discussion.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented eyelid irritation, redness, swelling, discharge, pain context, stye or hordeolum identity, typical eyelid-lump appearance, and selected risk-factor fields.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-led return, worsening, vision-change, severe-pain, and arranged follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral, worsening, vision change, severe redness, severe pain, photophobia, spread, trauma, uncertain diagnosis, and refractory-symptom documentation without automatically concluding urgency.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports specific visual, pain, photophobia, trauma, corneal, and pupil warning prompts only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports contact-lens history plus clinician-assessed visual-acuity, discharge, conjunctival, corneal, and pupil fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

unresolved_source_gaps: Tearing, crusting, itch, contact history, fixed duration presets, external-eye and extended eye examination, fluorescein, eye swab, contact-lens advice, medicine, fixed one-to-two-day review, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer a stye from eyelid swelling or recommend antibiotics, drops, incision, drainage, or referral.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
